---
name: reactjs-convention
description: "React.js conventions — project structure, naming rules, file organization, TypeScript usage, and import order. Use when: setting up a new project; deciding where to put files; naming components, hooks, or utilities."
---

# React.js Conventions Skill

## Overview

This skill covers the naming rules, file layout, and TypeScript conventions used across React projects. Follow these to keep the codebase consistent and easy to navigate.

---

## 1. Project Structure

```
src/
├── assets/          # Static assets (CSS, images)
├── components/      # Reusable UI components
│   ├── Button/
│   │   ├── Button.tsx
│   │   ├── Button.styled.ts  # Emotion styled components
│   │   ├── Button.test.tsx
│   │   ├── Button.stories.tsx
│   │   └── index.ts # Re-export
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all components
├── constants/       # Application constants
│   ├── apiConstants.ts
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all constants
├── forms/           # Formik form configs and Zod validation schemas
│   ├── loginSchema.ts
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all schemas
├── hooks/           # Custom React hooks
│   ├── useDebounce.ts
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all hooks
├── layouts/         # Layout components
│   ├── MainLayout.tsx
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all layouts
├── mutations/       # TanStack Query mutation hooks
│   ├── useCreateUser.ts
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all mutation hooks
├── pages/           # Page-level components (imported by route files)
│   ├── HomePage/
│   │   ├── HomePage.tsx
│   │   ├── HomePage.styled.ts  # Emotion styled components
│   │   ├── HomePage.test.tsx
│   │   └── index.ts # Re-export
│   ├── DashboardPage/
│   │   ├── DashboardPage.tsx
│   │   ├── DashboardPage.styled.ts
│   │   ├── DashboardPage.test.tsx
│   │   └── index.ts # Re-export
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all pages
├── providers/       # Context providers (ThemeProvider, AuthProvider, etc.)
│   ├── AuthProvider.tsx
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all providers
├── queries/         # TanStack Query query hooks
│   ├── useUsers.ts
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all query hooks
├── routes/          # TanStack Router route files
│   ├── __root.tsx   # Root layout
│   ├── index.tsx    # Home page (/)
│   ├── _auth.tsx    # Auth guard layout (pathless)
│   └── _auth/
│       └── dashboard.tsx
├── stores/          # Jotai atoms and derived atoms
│   ├── authAtom.ts
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all atoms
├── types/           # TypeScript type definitions
│   ├── user.ts
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all types
├── utils/           # Utility functions
│   ├── api.ts       # Axios base class
│   ├── authApi.ts   # Axios authenticated class
│   └── index.ts     # Re-export all utilities
├── i18n/            # i18next config and translation namespaces
│   ├── index.ts     # i18next initialization
│   └── locales/
│       ├── en/
│       │   └── common.json
│       └── vi/
│           └── common.json
├── routeTree.gen.ts # Auto-generated by TanStack Router — DO NOT EDIT
├── App.tsx
└── main.tsx
```

---

## 2. Naming Rules

| Item               | Convention                | Example                      |
| ------------------ | ------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| Component file     | PascalCase                | `UserCard.tsx`               |
| Component name     | PascalCase                | `function UserCard()`        |
| Hook file          | camelCase                 | `useAuth.ts`                 |
| Hook name          | `use` prefix              | `useAuth`, `useUserData`     |
| Utility file       | camelCase                 | `formatDate.ts`              |
| Atom file          | camelCase                 | `authAtom.ts`                |
| Atom variable      | `Atom` suffix             | `userAtom`, `isLoadingAtom`  |
| Query hook file    | camelCase                 | `useUsers.ts`                |
| Mutation hook file | camelCase                 | `useCreateUser.ts`           |
| Form schema file   | camelCase + Schema        | `loginSchema.ts`             |
| Provider file      | PascalCase                | `AuthProvider.tsx`           |
| Page file          | PascalCase + Page         | `DashboardPage.tsx`          |
| Route file         | TanStack file convention  | `_auth/dashboard.tsx`        |
| Constant file      | camelCase                 | `apiConstants.ts`            |
| Constant variable  | UPPER_SNAKE_CASE          | `MAX_RETRY_COUNT`            |
| Type / Interface   | PascalCase                | `UserProfile`, `ApiResponse` |
| Styled file        | PascalCase + `.styled.ts` | `Button.styled.ts`           |

---

## 3. File Rules

- **One component per file.** Do not put multiple exported components in one file.
- **Co-locate tests.** Keep `Button.test.tsx` next to `Button.tsx`.
- **Always create `index.ts`** in every folder to re-export its contents. This keeps imports short and makes refactoring easier.

### Components — subfolder index + top-level index

Every component lives in its own folder. The folder contains the component, its styles, tests, and stories.

```typescript
// components/Button/Button.styled.ts — Emotion styled components
import { styled } from "@mui/material/styles";

export const StyledButton = styled("button")(({ theme }) => ({
  padding: theme.spacing(1, 2),
  borderRadius: theme.shape.borderRadius,
}));

// components/Button/Button.tsx
import { StyledButton } from "./Button.styled";

export function Button({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return <StyledButton>{children}</StyledButton>;
}

// components/Button/index.ts — re-export from the subfolder
export { Button } from "./Button";
export type { ButtonProps } from "./Button";

// components/index.ts — re-export every component
export { Button } from "./Button";
export { UserCard } from "./UserCard";
```

```typescript
// Usage — single clean import path
import { Button, UserCard } from "@/components";
```

### Pages — same subfolder structure as components

Every page lives in its own folder with the same co-location pattern.

```typescript
// pages/HomePage/HomePage.styled.ts
import { styled } from "@mui/material/styles";

export const HeroSection = styled("section")(({ theme }) => ({
  padding: theme.spacing(4),
}));

// pages/HomePage/HomePage.tsx
import { HeroSection } from "./HomePage.styled";

export function HomePage() {
  return <HeroSection><h1>Home</h1></HeroSection>;
}

// pages/HomePage/index.ts
export { HomePage } from "./HomePage";

// pages/index.ts — re-export every page
export { HomePage } from "./HomePage";
export { DashboardPage } from "./DashboardPage";
```

### Queries, mutations, hooks, stores, types, utils, constants, forms

Each of these folders must also have an `index.ts` that re-exports everything inside.

```typescript
// queries/index.ts
export { useUsers } from "./useUsers";
export { useUser } from "./useUser";

// mutations/index.ts
export { useCreateUser } from "./useCreateUser";
export { useDeleteUser } from "./useDeleteUser";

// stores/index.ts
export { tokenAtom } from "./authAtom";
export { userAtom } from "./userAtom";

// types/index.ts
export type { User, ApiResponse } from "./user";

// constants/index.ts
export { API_BASE_URL, MAX_RETRY_COUNT } from "./apiConstants";

// utils/index.ts
export { api } from "./api";
export { authApi } from "./authApi";

// hooks/index.ts
export { useDebounce } from "./useDebounce";
export { useLocalStorage } from "./useLocalStorage";

// forms/index.ts
export { loginInitialValues, loginValidationSchema } from "./loginSchema";
```

```typescript
// Usage — short, consistent imports everywhere
import { useUsers } from "@/queries";
import { useCreateUser } from "@/mutations";
import { tokenAtom } from "@/stores";
import { authApi } from "@/utils";
import type { User } from "@/types";
```

---

## 4. TypeScript Conventions

```typescript
// ✅ Use interface for component props
interface UserCardProps {
  userId: string;
  name: string;
  avatarUrl?: string;  // Optional with ?
  onClick: (id: string) => void;
}

// ✅ Export props type alongside component
export function UserCard({ userId, name, avatarUrl, onClick }: UserCardProps) {
  return (
    <div onClick={() => onClick(userId)}>
      <img src={avatarUrl} alt={name} />
      <span>{name}</span>
    </div>
  );
}

// ✅ Use React.FC only when you need implicit children typing (rare)
// Prefer plain function with typed props instead
```

```typescript
// ✅ Type API responses in types/
// src/types/user.ts
export interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role: "admin" | "user";
}

export interface ApiResponse<T> {
  data: T;
  message: string;
  success: boolean;
}
```

---

## 5. Import Order

Group imports in this order, separated by blank lines:

```typescript
// 1. React and framework
import { useState, useEffect } from "react";

// 2. Third-party libraries
import { useAtom } from "jotai";
import { useNavigate, Link } from "@tanstack/react-router";
import { Box, Button, Typography } from "@mui/material";
import { useTranslation } from "react-i18next";
import dayjs from "dayjs";

// 3. Internal absolute imports (@/) — always import from the folder index
import { useUsers } from "@/queries";
import { useCreateUser } from "@/mutations";
import { userAtom } from "@/stores";
import { API_BASE_URL } from "@/constants";
import type { User } from "@/types";

// 4. Relative imports
import { UserAvatar } from "./UserAvatar";
```

---

## 6. Path Aliases

Always configure `@/` pointing to `src/` to avoid long relative paths:

```json
// tsconfig.json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "baseUrl": ".",
    "paths": {
      "@/*": ["src/*"]
    }
  }
}
```

```typescript
// vite.config.ts
import path from "path";
export default {
  resolve: {
    alias: { "@": path.resolve(__dirname, "src") },
  },
};
```
